name: Trishula Full
description: The Trishula, meaning 'three spear' or 'trident', is a divine weapon and symbol prominently associated with the Hindu deity Shiva. It represents the three fundamental aspects of existence: creation, preservation, and destruction. Each prong of the Trishula signifies a specific power, reflecting Shiva's multifaceted nature. The central prong represents equilibrium and balance, while the other two represent opposing forces that Shiva controls and harmonizes. The Trishula is not merely a weapon of destruction; it symbolizes the destruction of ignorance, ego, and illusion. It is a powerful emblem of Shiva's authority, representing his control over time, space, and causation. Depictions often show Shiva holding the Trishula, signifying his role as the destroyer of evil and the protector of the righteous. The Trishula is also associated with the three Gunas – Sattva (goodness, purity), Rajas (passion, activity), and Tamas (ignorance, inertia) – which are the fundamental qualities of nature. Shiva, wielding the Trishula, transcends these qualities and embodies pure consciousness. The weapon is also connected to the control over the three aspects of time: past, present, and future. Its presence is a reminder of the cyclical nature of existence and the impermanence of all things. The Trishula's significance extends beyond its association with Shiva, often appearing as a symbol of power and authority for other deities and spiritual figures in Hindu iconography. The Trishula embodies the power to overcome obstacles and achieve spiritual liberation.
subtitle: Shiva's powerful three-pronged trident.
currentLocation: Held by Shiva or present in Shiva temples.

keyMyths.title: Shiva's Weapon
keyMyths.description: The Trishula is Shiva's primary weapon, used to destroy evil and maintain cosmic balance. It signifies his power over creation, preservation, and destruction.

keyMyths.title: Durga's Trishula
keyMyths.description: Durga is sometimes depicted wielding a Trishula, showcasing her power and strength in defeating demons. This symbolizes her role as a protector and destroyer of evil forces.

sources.author: Shiva Purana
sources.description: A Hindu text primarily focusing on the deity Shiva.
sources.text: A Hindu text primarily focusing on the deity Shiva.

sources.author: Devi Mahatmya
sources.description: A Hindu text describing the Goddess Durga's victory over demons.
